004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.lang.reflect.*;
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
026
027/**
028 * Canonical home for object-level helpers: null/empty checks, equality, comparison,
029 * coalescing, boolean folds, identity/stringify, small numeric helpers, and optionals.
030 *
031 * <p>Terse aliases for these methods live in {@link Shorts}.
032 */
033@SuppressWarnings({
034   "java:S1118" // Utility class with static methods only.
035})
036public class ObjectUtils {
037
038   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
039
040   /** Constructor — this class is meant to be subclassed. */
041   protected ObjectUtils() {}
042
043   /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the object is not <jk>null</jk>. */
044   public static boolean isNotNull(Object o) { return o != null; }
045
046   /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the object is <jk>null</jk>. */
047   public static <T> boolean isNull(T value) { return value == null; }
048
049   /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if all specified values are <jk>null</jk> (or the array is <jk>null</jk>). */
050   public static boolean isNull(Object...values) {
051      if (values == null) return true;
052      for (var v : values) if (v != null) return false;
053      return true;
054   }
055
056   /** Returns <jk>null</jk> typed as the specified class (type-inference helper). */
057   @SuppressWarnings({
058      "java:S1172", // Parameter used for type inference only.
059      "unused"      // Parameter used for type inference only.
060   })
061   public static <T> T nullObject(Class<T> type) { return null; }
062
063   /** Null/array/annotation-aware equality. */
064   public static <T> boolean equal(T o1, T o2) {
065      if (o1 instanceof java.lang.annotation.Annotation o1a && o2 instanceof java.lang.annotation.Annotation o2a)
066         return AnnotationUtils.equals(o1a, o2a);
067      if (ClassUtils.isArray(o1) && ClassUtils.isArray(o2)) {
068         var l1 = Array.getLength(o1);
069         var l2 = Array.getLength(o2);
070         if (l1 != l2) return false;
071         for (var i = 0; i < l1; i++)
072            if (notEqual(Array.get(o1, i), Array.get(o2, i))) return false;
073         return true;
074      }
075      return Objects.equals(o1, o2);
076   }
077
078   /** String equality with optional case-insensitivity. */
079   public static boolean equal(boolean caseInsensitive, String s1, String s2) {
080      return caseInsensitive ? equalIgnoreCase(s1, s2) : equal(s1, s2);
081   }
082
083   /** Null-safe equality via a custom predicate. */
084   public static <T,U> boolean equal(T o1, U o2, BiPredicate<T,U> test) {
085      if (o1 == null) return o2 == null;
086      if (o2 == null) return false;
087      if (o1 == o2) return true;
088      return test.test(o1, o2);
089   }
090
091   /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the first argument equals any of the varargs. */
092   @SafeVarargs
093   public static <T> boolean equalsAny(T o1, T...o2) {
094      if (o2 == null || o2.length == 0) return false;
095      for (var o : o2) if (equal(o1, o)) return true;
096      return false;
097   }
098
099   /** Case-insensitive equality of two objects' string forms. */
100   public static boolean equalIgnoreCase(Object o1, Object o2) { return StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase(o1, o2); }
101
102   /** Inverse of {@link #equal(Object,Object)}. */
103   public static <T> boolean notEqual(T o1, T o2) { return ! equal(o1, o2); }
104
105   /** Inverse of {@link #equal(Object,Object,BiPredicate)}. */
106   public static <T,U> boolean notEqual(T o1, U o2, BiPredicate<T,U> test) {
107      if (o1 == null) return isNotNull(o2);
108      if (o2 == null) return true;
109      if (o1 == o2) return false;
110      return ! test.test(o1, o2);
111   }
112
113   /** Null-tolerant natural-order compare (0 if types differ / not Comparable). */
114   @SuppressWarnings({
115      "unchecked",   // Type erasure requires unchecked casts.
116      "java:S3740"   // Raw Comparable; parameterizing breaks compareTo.
117   })
118   public static int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {
119      if (o1 == null) return o2 == null ? 0 : -1;
120      if (o2 == null) return 1;
121      if (equal(o1.getClass(), o2.getClass()) && o1 instanceof Comparable o12)
122         return o12.compareTo(o2);
123      return 0;
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if o1 is less than o2.
128    *
129    * @param <T> The comparable type.
130    * @param o1 Object 1.
131    * @param o2 Object 2.
132    * @return <jk>true</jk> if o1 is less than o2.
133    */
134   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lessThan(T o1, T o2) {
135      if (o1 == null) return o2 != null;
136      if (o2 == null) return false;
137      return o1.compareTo(o2) < 0;
138   }
139
140   /**
141    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if o1 is less than or equal to o2.
142    *
143    * @param <T> The comparable type.
144    * @param o1 Object 1.
145    * @param o2 Object 2.
146    * @return <jk>true</jk> if o1 is less than or equal to o2.
147    */
148   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean lessThanOrEqual(T o1, T o2) {
149      if (o1 == null) return true;
150      if (o2 == null) return false;
151      return o1.compareTo(o2) <= 0;
152   }
153
154   /**
155    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if o1 is greater than o2.
156    *
157    * @param <T> The comparable type.
158    * @param o1 Object 1.
159    * @param o2 Object 2.
160    * @return <jk>true</jk> if o1 is greater than o2.
161    */
162   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean greaterThan(T o1, T o2) {
163      if (o1 == null) return false;
164      if (o2 == null) return true;
165      return o1.compareTo(o2) > 0;
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if o1 is greater than or equal to o2.
170    *
171    * @param <T> The comparable type.
172    * @param o1 Object 1.
173    * @param o2 Object 2.
174    * @return <jk>true</jk> if o1 is greater than or equal to o2.
175    */
176   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> boolean greaterThanOrEqual(T o1, T o2) {
177      if (o1 == null) return o2 == null;
178      if (o2 == null) return true;
179      return o1.compareTo(o2) >= 0;
180   }
181
182   /**
183    * Returns the minimum of two comparable values.
184    *
185    * @param <T> The comparable type.
186    * @param o1 Value 1.
187    * @param o2 Value 2.
188    * @return The minimum value.
189    */
190   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> T min(T o1, T o2) {
191      if (o1 == null) return o2;
192      if (o2 == null) return o1;
193      return o1.compareTo(o2) <= 0 ? o1 : o2;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Returns the maximum of two comparable values.
198    *
199    * @param <T> The comparable type.
200    * @param o1 Value 1.
201    * @param o2 Value 2.
202    * @return The maximum value.
203    */
204   public static <T extends Comparable<T>> T max(T o1, T o2) {
205      if (o1 == null) return o2;
206      if (o2 == null) return o1;
207      return o1.compareTo(o2) >= 0 ? o1 : o2;
208   }
209
210   /** Returns the value if non-null, else the default (coalesce). */
211   public static <T> T coalesce(T value, T defaultValue) { return isNotNull(value) ? value : defaultValue; }
212
213   /** Returns the first non-null value, or <jk>null</jk>. */
214   @SafeVarargs
215   public static <T> T coalesce(T...t) {
216      if (isNotNull(t)) for (var tt : t) if (isNotNull(tt)) return tt;
217      return null;
218   }
219
220   /** Boolean AND-fold (vacuously true when empty). */
221   public static boolean allTrue(boolean...values) {
222      if (values == null || values.length == 0) return true;
223      for (var v : values) if (! v) return false;
224      return true;
225   }
226
227   /** Boolean OR-fold (false when empty). */
228   public static boolean anyTrue(boolean...values) {
229      if (values == null || values.length == 0) return false;
230      for (var v : values) if (v) return true;
231      return false;
232   }
233
234   /** Boolean negation. */
235   public static boolean negate(boolean value) { return ! value; }
236
237   /** Null-safe {@link Boolean#TRUE} check. */
238   public static boolean isTrue(Object value) { return Boolean.TRUE.equals(value); }
239
240   /** Null-safe {@link Boolean#FALSE} check. */
241   public static boolean isFalse(Object value) { return Boolean.FALSE.equals(value); }
242
243   /** Coerces an object to a boolean (false if null). */
244   public static boolean toBoolean(Object val) {
245      return optional(val).map(Object::toString).map(Boolean::valueOf).orElse(false);
246   }
247
248   /** Null-safe toString (returns <jk>null</jk> for <jk>null</jk>). */
249   public static String stringify(Object val) { return val == null ? null : val.toString(); }
250
251   /** "SimpleQualifiedName@identityHashCode" for the object (unwrapping Optionals). */
252   public static String identity(Object o) {
253      if (o instanceof Optional<?> opt) o = opt.orElse(null);
254      if (o == null) return null;
255      return ClassUtils.classNameSimpleQualified(o) + "@" + System.identityHashCode(o);
256   }
257
258   /** Unwraps a Supplier/Holder/Optional to its inner value. */
259   public static Object unwrap(Object o) {
260      if (o instanceof Supplier<?> o2) o = unwrap(o2.get());
261      if (o instanceof Holder<?> o2) o = unwrap(o2.get());
262      if (o instanceof Optional<?> o2) o = unwrap(o2.orElse(null));
263      return o;
264   }
265
266   /** Absolute value across Number subtypes. */
267   @SuppressWarnings({ "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked cast for Number types.
268   })
269   public static <T extends Number> T abs(T value) {
270      if (value == null) return null;
271      if (value instanceof Integer) return (T)Integer.valueOf(Math.abs(value.intValue()));
272      if (value instanceof Long) return (T)Long.valueOf(Math.abs(value.longValue()));
273      if (value instanceof Double) return (T)Double.valueOf(Math.abs(value.doubleValue()));
274      if (value instanceof Float) return (T)Float.valueOf(Math.abs(value.floatValue()));
275      if (value instanceof Short) return (T)Short.valueOf((short)Math.abs(value.shortValue()));
276      if (value instanceof Byte) return (T)Byte.valueOf((byte)Math.abs(value.byteValue()));
277      return (T)Double.valueOf(Math.abs(value.doubleValue()));
278   }
279
280   /** Inclusive between. */
281   public static boolean isBetween(int n, int lower, int higher) { return n >= lower && n <= higher; }
282
283   /** True if the number is non-null and not -1. */
284   public static <T extends Number> boolean isNotMinusOne(T value) { return isNotNull(value) && value.intValue() != -1; }
285
286   /** Content/annotation/array-aware hash. */
287   public static int hash(Object...values) {
288      assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
289      return HashCode.of(values);
290   }
291
292   /** True if the object (String/Collection/Map/array/other) is null or empty. */
293   public static boolean isEmpty(Object o) {
294      if (o == null) return true;
295      if (o instanceof Collection<?> o2) return o2.isEmpty();
296      if (o instanceof Map<?,?> o2) return o2.isEmpty();
297      if (ClassUtils.isArray(o)) return Array.getLength(o) == 0;
298      return o.toString().isEmpty();
299   }
300
301   /** Inverse of {@link #isEmpty(Object)}. */
302   public static boolean isNotEmpty(Object value) {
303      if (value == null) return false;
304      if (value instanceof CharSequence value2) return ! value2.isEmpty();
305      if (value instanceof Collection<?> value2) return ! value2.isEmpty();
306      if (value instanceof Map<?,?> value2) return ! value2.isEmpty();
307      if (ClassUtils.isArray(value)) return Array.getLength(value) > 0;
308      return isNotEmpty(stringify(value));
309   }
310
311   /** {@link Optional#ofNullable(Object)} shorthand. */
312   public static <T> Optional<T> optional(T t) { return Optional.ofNullable(t); }
313
314   /** {@link Optional#empty()} shorthand. */
315   public static <T> Optional<T> emptyOptional() { return Optional.empty(); }
316
317   /** {@code optional.orElse(defaultValue)} shorthand. */
318   public static <T> T orElse(Optional<T> optional, T defaultValue) { return optional.orElse(defaultValue); }
319
320   /** Asserts non-null and returns the value. */
321   public static <T> T requireNonNull(T value) { return Objects.requireNonNull(value); }
322
323   /** Null-safe size (0 for null; length/size for String/Collection/Map/array; else 1). */
324   public static int size(Object o) {
325      if (o == null) return 0;
326      if (o instanceof CharSequence o2) return o2.length();
327      if (o instanceof Collection<?> o2) return o2.size();
328      if (o instanceof Map<?,?> o2) return o2.size();
329      if (ClassUtils.isArray(o)) return Array.getLength(o);
330      return 1;
331   }
332}
